question: Explain the relationship between the main thread and other threads in a Java application. How does the termination of the main thread affect other threads?
expectedPoints: The main thread is created automatically when a Java program starts
It is the thread from which other "child" threads are spawned
Termination of the main thread does NOT automatically terminate other threads
Other threads can continue to execute after the main thread ends
A Java application continues to run until all non-daemon threads have completed

question: Describe the purpose and typical use of the main thread in a Java program.
expectedPoints: Entry point for application execution (main() method)
Used to initialize the application and create GUI components
Often responsible for spawning additional worker threads
Handles the primary sequence of operations at startup
Can be used for high-priority or UI-update tasks in some frameworks

question: Compare and contrast daemon threads and user threads in Java, with specific emphasis on how the main thread relates to each type.
expectedPoints: User threads prevent the JVM from exiting while they're running
Daemon threads do not prevent JVM termination
The main thread is a user thread
All threads created by the main thread inherit its daemon status unless explicitly set
JVM exits when only daemon threads remain
